buggy_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from itertools import product

prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)
returns = prices['close'].pct_change().dropna()

best_sharpe = -np.inf
best_params = None

# Grid search over ALL data
for short, long_ in product(range(5, 50), range(20, 200)):
    if short >= long_:
        continue
    sma_short = prices['close'].rolling(short).mean().shift(1)
    sma_long = prices['close'].rolling(long_).mean().shift(1)
    signal = (sma_short > sma_long).astype(int)
    strategy_returns = signal * returns
    sharpe = strategy_returns.mean() / strategy_returns.std() * np.sqrt(252)
    if sharpe > best_sharpe:
        best_sharpe = sharpe
        best_params = (short, long_)

print(f'Best params: {best_params}, Sharpe: {best_sharpe:.2f}')
clean_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from itertools import product

prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)
returns = prices['close'].pct_change().dropna()

# Split into train and test BEFORE optimization
split = int(len(prices) * 0.7)
train_prices = prices.iloc[:split]
train_returns = returns.iloc[:split]
test_prices = prices.iloc[split:]
test_returns = returns.iloc[split:]

best_sharpe = -np.inf
best_params = None

# Grid search on TRAINING data only
for short, long_ in product(range(5, 50), range(20, 200)):
    if short >= long_:
        continue
    sma_short = train_prices['close'].rolling(short).mean().shift(1)
    sma_long = train_prices['close'].rolling(long_).mean().shift(1)
    signal = (sma_short > sma_long).astype(int)
    strategy_returns = signal * train_returns
    sharpe = strategy_returns.mean() / strategy_returns.std() * np.sqrt(252)
    if sharpe > best_sharpe:
        best_sharpe = sharpe
        best_params = (short, long_)

# Evaluate best params on UNSEEN test data
short, long_ = best_params
sma_short = test_prices['close'].rolling(short).mean().shift(1)
sma_long = test_prices['close'].rolling(long_).mean().shift(1)
signal = (sma_short > sma_long).astype(int)
test_sharpe = (signal * test_returns).mean() / (signal * test_returns).std() * np.sqrt(252)
print(f'Out-of-sample Sharpe: {test_sharpe:.2f}')
description: Strategy parameters optimized on entire dataset and reported as performance.
explanation: Optimizing parameters on all data and then reporting performance on the same data is pure overfitting. The best_sharpe reported is the in-sample optimum, not a realistic estimate of live performance. Any real deployment will see dramatically worse results.
suggested_fix: Always optimize parameters on training data only. Report performance on a held-out test set that was not used during optimization.

issues.title: Parameter optimization on full dataset overfits in-sample
